zh.title: 以太坊/美元技术分析：短线震荡偏强延续
zh.content:
以太坊短线呈窄幅震荡偏强结构，价格运行在1730上方并贴近日内高点，整体仍维持低波动整理格局，趋势略偏多但动能有限延续结构延续中

MACD指标维持正值区域，动能柱略有收敛但仍支持多头结构，均线系统中价格站稳SMA20上方，短期趋势偏向温和上行但需关注回落风险

上方阻力关注1744及1755区域，下方支撑位于1730与SMA20附近，若跌破则可能回测1700整数关口，区间结构清晰短线震荡为主等待突破方向确认信号

交易策略建议以区间操作为主，回踩1730-1716可分批布局多单，若放量突破1744则顺势跟进，严格控制风险仓位避免追高追空等待确认信号。

en.title: Ethereum vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
ETH/USD is currently trading in a tight consolidation phase after a mild 24-hour decline of 0.17%, holding near 1733. Price action remains relatively stable, with sellers failing to extend momentum below the intraday low around 1732. The broader structure still leans slightly bullish, as price continues to hover above the 20-period simple moving average, suggesting underlying demand despite short-term hesitation near local highs.

MACD shows a positive reading at 19.12494, indicating that bullish momentum is still present, although the absence of a visible signal line limits precise crossover confirmation. With RSI unavailable, momentum assessment relies more heavily on price structure, which suggests a neutral-to-slightly bullish bias. The market appears to be coiling, with reduced volatility hinting at a potential breakout attempt if volume expands. Intraday structure favors cautious bullish continuation while range compression persists.

Immediate support is located near 1732, aligning with the session low, followed closely by the 20-day SMA around 1716, which serves as a stronger dynamic support zone. A break below this level could expose the 1700 psychological mark and deeper liquidity around 1680. On the upside, resistance is found at 1744, with a breakout potentially targeting 1755 to 1770 if momentum strengthens.

Short-term traders may consider range-bound strategies, buying near support zones and taking partial profits into resistance, given the current sideways structure. Breakout traders should wait for a confirmed close above 1744 or below 1716 before committing to directional positions. Risk management remains essential, with tight stops recommended due to compressed volatility and the potential for sudden expansion in either direction.
